assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md classifies comment 30001 as 'Code Change Request' with action 'Create sub-task (subtask-30001.md)'. The report's Review Comments Summary table confirms classification as 'Code change request' with 'Sub-task Created: Yes (subtask-30001.md)'. subtask-30001.md exists and contains the sub-task.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as a code change request (or upgraded suggestion) and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md classifies comment 30002 as 'Code Change Request (upgraded from suggestion)' with a detailed Convention Upgrade Analysis section. Action is 'Create sub-task (subtask-30002.md)'. The report confirms classification as 'Code change request (upgraded from suggestion)' with sub-task created. subtask-30002.md exists.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md classifies comment 30003 as 'Nit' with action 'No sub-task created. This is a nit-level comment that does not warrant a follow-up task.' The report's Review Comments Summary shows classification as 'Nit' with 'Sub-task Created: No'. No subtask-30003.md file exists.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md classifies comment 30004 as 'Question' with action 'No sub-task created. This is a question requiring author/team clarification, not an actionable code change request.' The report's Review Comments Summary shows classification as 'Question' with 'Sub-task Created: No'. No subtask-30004.md file exists.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (trustify-backend), '## Description' (wrap UPDATE statements in transaction), '## Files to Modify' (sbom.rs), '## Implementation Notes' (SeaORM transaction API usage), '## Acceptance Criteria' (4 items). subtask-30002.md also contains all required sections: '## Repository' (trustify-backend), '## Description' (add partial index), '## Files to Modify' (migration mod.rs), '## Implementation Notes' (SQL and SeaORM patterns), '## Acceptance Criteria' (3 items).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' section with URL 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744'. subtask-30002.md contains '## Target PR' section with URL 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744'.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' section with the full original comment text from reviewer-a about wrapping three UPDATE statements in a transaction. subtask-30002.md contains '## Review Context' section with the full original comment text from reviewer-a about adding an index on deleted_at.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs consist only of analysis/report files: report.md, review-30001.md through review-30004.md, and subtask-30001.md and subtask-30002.md. No code files were modified. The report analyzes code via reading (evidence of diff analysis and file inspection), but all outputs are documentation/analysis artifacts, not code changes.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) are individually classified in separate review files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report's Review Comments Summary table lists all four comments with their classifications. The Findings section references both actionable comments (30001, 30002) and acknowledges the other two (30003 as observation, 30004 as scope observation). All comments were processed.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report verdict is 'FAIL -- Changes Requested by Reviewer' and states 'These must be addressed before the PR can be approved.' There is no mention of merging, auto-merge, or any merge action anywhere in the report. Two sub-tasks were created for the requested changes, indicating the PR workflow continues rather than concluding with a merge.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: The report contains a '## Test Change Classification' section with '**Classification**: ADDITIVE' and states 'All test changes are in a **new test file** (`tests/api/sbom_delete.rs`). No existing test files were modified.'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a detailed '### Convention Upgrade Analysis' section evaluating four convention alignment factors: (1) Database performance convention — indexes on columns used in frequent WHERE clauses, (2) Partial index specificity — reviewer provides exact SQL, (3) Production impact — list query performance degradation, (4) Migration completeness convention — column and index should be in same migration. The analysis concludes the suggestion qualifies for upgrade to code change request based on convention alignment.

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path — whether classified directly as code change request based on reviewer language, or upgraded from suggestion via convention analysis
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md classifies comment 30002 as 'Code Change Request (upgraded from suggestion)' and the action is 'Create sub-task (subtask-30002.md)'. subtask-30002.md exists with full sub-task content including Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Target PR, and Review Context sections. The sub-task was created via the convention upgrade path.
